title: Principal Product Manager, Voice of Community
raw_jd:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


The Voice of Community (VoComm) team part of Community Operations enables the community to have an ”empty chair“ in the room as a reminder that we start from them and work backwards. We earn the trust of the community by amplifying their voice, transforming it into actionable insights, and building products that empower Amazonians to innovate on behalf of the customer.  

  

We are looking for a Principal Product Manager who is excited about making a difference in our communities. In this role, you will be the strategic driver behind how we listen to, understand, and act on community insights across our product portfolio. You will champion a "Community as Customer" mindset throughout the organization while building products and frameworks that amplify the voice of our community in every product decision we make. You will develop and drive high\-level strategic initiatives but will also be willing to roll up your sleeves, dig in and get the job done.
  

  

Key job responsibilities  

Strategic Vision \& Roadmap  

* Develop and articulate a compelling short\-term and long\-term product vision for Voice of Community initiatives that aligns with company objectives
* Build and maintain a cross\-product roadmap that integrates community feedback mechanisms across all product lines
* Define success metrics and KPIs that measure the impact of community\-driven product development

  

Executive Stakeholder Management  

* Build and nurture strong relationships with Directors, Vice Presidents, and other senior leaders across the organization
* Present strategic recommendations and product proposals to executive leadership
* Influence and align cross\-functional stakeholders around Voice of Community priorities

  

Product Adoption \& Evangelism  

* Drive adoption of Voice of Community products, tools, and methodologies across user teams
* Create compelling narratives and business cases that demonstrate the value of community insights
* Develop training programs and enablement materials to empower teams to leverage community feedback
* Track and communicate adoption metrics, success stories, and ROI to stakeholders

  

Community as Customer Mindset  

* Champion and embed a "Community as Customer" philosophy throughout the product organization
* Establish best practices, frameworks, and playbooks for incorporating user feedback into product development
* Partner with research, data science, and analytics teams to build robust community listening capabilities
* Foster a culture of community empathy and customer\-centricity across all teams

  

Cross\-Functional Leadership  

* Mentor and guide product managers on community\-centric product development approaches
* Represent the voice of the community in cross organization reviews, planning sessions, and strategic discussions
**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* Bachelor's degree
* Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
* 10\+ years of blending product and program management skills to execute strategic initiatives about process creation, standardization, and improvement experience
* 10\+ years of end to end product delivery experience
* Experience in data analysis and leveraging analytics to make decisions

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, analytics, mathematics, statistics, IT or equivalent

  

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.  

The base salary range for this position is listed below. Your Amazon package will include sign\-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon also offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, Basic Life \& AD\&D insurance and option for Supplemental life plans, EAP, Mental Health Support, Medical Advice Line, Flexible Spending Accounts, Adoption and Surrogacy Reimbursement coverage), 401(k) matching, paid time off, and parental leave. Learn more about our benefits at https://amazon.jobs/en/benefits.

  

USA, TN, Nashville \- 145,700\.00 \- 197,100\.00 USD annually  

USA, VA, Arlington \- 161,900\.00 \- 219,000\.00 USD annually  

USA, WA, Bellevue \- 161,900\.00 \- 219,000\.00 USD annually

score.skills_rationale: Moderate skills overlap exists through data analysis, analytics, KPI development, and strategic initiative experience. However, this role emphasizes product management, community engagement, and cross-functional leadership - areas where the candidate has some experience but not as a primary focus. The candidate's deep technical BI/analytics background doesn't directly translate to product management responsibilities.
score.scope_rationale: Role scope matches candidate's senior level with executive stakeholder management, strategic vision, and mentoring responsibilities. However, this is a pure product management role requiring community-focused product development experience, which differs significantly from the candidate's data/analytics architecture background. The leadership expectations align but in a different functional domain.
score.pay_rationale: Arlington salary range of $161,900-$219,000 aligns well with candidate's W2 target of $160K-$220K, showing good compensation match.
red_flags: Major functional pivot from data/analytics to product management
No clear product management experience in background
score.raw_analysis: This is a senior product management role at Amazon requiring extensive PM experience and community-focused product development expertise. While the candidate has strong analytical skills and some strategic leadership experience, their background is primarily in data/analytics architecture rather than product management. The role requires driving product adoption, managing cross-functional teams in a product context, and deep community engagement skills that don't align with the candidate's technical BI/data focus. Despite good pay alignment and appropriate seniority level, the functional mismatch is too significant.
